methodsAttempted.query: site:bjtu.edu.cn "生成式人工智能" "北京交通大学"
methodsAttempted.notes: Results were faculty profiles, research/news pages, and an Undergraduate School news item about a teaching AI-agent application case; these were not used for binding policy claims.
Verified www.bjtu.edu.cn and bksy.bjtu.edu.cn as official Beijing Jiaotong University domains by site branding and bjtu.edu.cn institutional domain control.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found one primary official PDF with AI-related undergraduate teaching and curriculum evidence. Official searches did not locate a binding university-wide generative-AI use policy, so staged claims are limited to teaching/curriculum practices described in the Undergraduate School quality report.

targets.sourceTitle: 北京交通大学2023-2024学年本科教学质量报告

stopConditions: Stop after the official Undergraduate School quality report supports undergraduate teaching and AI-curriculum claims.
Do not infer binding student AI-use rules, academic-integrity sanctions, privacy rules, procurement approvals, or university-wide tool policy from teaching quality report text.
Do not use official news recognition pages, faculty profiles, research pages, or search snippets as claim evidence.
Do not stage raw PDF, HTML, screenshots, or full normalized text in Git.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 北京交通大学2023-2024学年本科教学质量报告

artifacts.claimText: Beijing Jiaotong University's 2023-2024 undergraduate teaching quality report says the university opened liberal-arts/science-track AI general courses and aimed for AI course coverage for the 2024 undergraduate cohort, except where programs already include AI courses.
artifacts.normalizedValue: 2024 undergraduate cohort AI-course coverage through AI general foundation/application courses, with duplicate courses avoided where programs already include AI courses.

artifacts.claimText: For the Zhan Tianyou College-related 2024 undergraduate training model described in the report, BJTU required majors in the information ability module to add AI-specialized courses categorized as AI foundation, AI expansion, and AI frontier courses.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Zhan Tianyou College 2024 training model information ability module includes AI-specialized courses in foundation, expansion, and frontier categories.

artifacts.claimText: BJTU's undergraduate teaching quality report says the university developed AI-enabled course pilots using large-model technologies around knowledge graphs, 24-hour intelligent learning companions, AI teaching assistants, and AI mentors, with experience to be promoted across the university.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 北京交通大学2023-2024学年本科教学质量报告
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 为提高学生数字素养，按文科、理工科分类开设“人工智能通识基础”“人工智能基础及应用”，专业课程已包含人工智能类课程则无需重复开设，做到 2024 级人工智能课程全覆盖；
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: To improve students' digital literacy, BJTU opened AI general foundation and AI foundation/application courses by humanities/science tracks, avoiding repeats where programs already include AI courses, to cover the 2024 cohort.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: PDF text near section 3, lines extracted around general education resources

artifacts.title: Beijing Jiaotong University AI Teaching Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: The staged evidence for Beijing Jiaotong University is limited to official Undergraduate School teaching-quality-report statements about AI-related undergraduate courses, AI-specialized curriculum modules, and AI-enabled course pilots. No binding university-wide generative-AI policy was found in official-source searches.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The source supports teaching and curriculum claims only; it does not establish binding AI-use, academic-integrity, privacy, procurement, or approved-tool rules.
Official-source searches found low-specificity AI news and research/faculty pages but no primary university-wide generative-AI policy.
